mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate on equity cash flows and signals easier financial conditions, so the S&P 500 should rise over the next month when policy is actively easing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are not positive in a majority of periods with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation runs hot while FEDFUNDS remains low, real policy rates fall and inflation uncertainty rises; equities reprice downward over the next 20 trading days as the market expects future tightening.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds FEDFUNDS by at least 2 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not show negative 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the interaction term adds no predictive value versus CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening household income and future demand, so equity prices should underperform over the next month when unemployment is increasing from already elevated levels.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of unemployment-rise triggers, or if the signal's IC is not negative in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth in PAYEMS improves aggregate income and consumption prospects, supporting risk assets; sustained labor-market expansion should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day SPX forward returns are not above baseline after positive PAYEMS momentum, or if the signal flips sign across repeated rolling samples.

mechanism_narrative: Higher RSAFS implies stronger current spending and revenue growth, which should lift earnings expectations and push the S&P 500 higher over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum is followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the predictive relationship disappears after controlling for recent market trend.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO signals weakening industrial output and profit margins, so equities should fall over the next 20 trading days when production momentum turns negative.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not negative after two-month INDPRO declines, or if the effect is absent in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST and RSAFS both weaken together, the signal identifies a synchronized demand slowdown that should lead the S&P 500 lower over the next month because both housing activity and retail spending are decelerating.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both decline on a 3-month basis
failure_condition: Reject if joint HOUST/RSAFS declines do not outperform each series alone in predicting negative 20-day SPX returns, or if hit-rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle with CPIAUCSL still accelerating, rate hikes amplify valuation pressure and recession risk, so the S&P 500 should underperform over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises while CPIAUCSL 6-month inflation remains above 4%
failure_condition: Reject if tightening-plus-high-inflation episodes do not produce negative 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the effect weakens to zero in any 3-year rolling window.
